title: Defaults registry — per-field safe-default policies
description: A3 consults this registry to decide DEFAULT_APPLIED vs NEEDS_CLARIFICATION. Per Phase-1 design: materiality lives in this registry, not in A3's logic. If a field has an entry with defaultable=true, A3 may auto-apply. Otherwise A3 must ask the user.

fields.description: Map of field identifier (matches A1 ambiguity_flags or A2 default_candidates.field) to its policy.

defaultable.description: If true, A3 may auto-apply default_value. If false, A3 must route to NEEDS_CLARIFICATION.

default_value.description: The value A3 applies when defaultable=true. May be string, number, boolean, or object depending on the field.

candidates.description: Alternative values that could also be reasonable. When defaultable=true, the first entry equals default_value. When defaultable=false, candidates feed A3's clarification options.

no_default_reason.description: Required when defaultable=false. Factual explanation of why the answer can change materially based on the user's pick.

disclosure_template.description: Template for the user-facing disclosure when this default is applied. Placeholders: {chosen}, {field_human_name}. Example: 'Showing {chosen} (no time range specified).'

applies_to_report_types.description: Optional scope. When set, policy applies only to listed report types. Otherwise applies to all.

depends_on.description: Optional. When set, the default depends on another field's value (e.g. comparison_period_implicit default = prior_period_matched_length, where 'matched_length' depends on current period choice).

registry_recommended.description: Optional. When true and defaultable=false, A3 may surface the first candidate as a 'recommended' hint in clarification UI — but still asks the user.
